title: Veteran Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Veteran Support Groups 10 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups and Alcohol Anonymous meetings. 65920 SW 61st Street Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Must be a veteran Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 706-9062 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals access benefits and services earned through military service and provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) and other government agencies such as healthcare, disability compensation, education, employment, and financial assistance. Provides additional support services like job finding assistance, Medicare information and counseling, transportation to medical appointments, emergency financial grants, support groups, volunteer opportunities, advocacy, and more. 100 SW Main Street Room 212a Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Veterans or dependents of veterans, with a focus on those with disabilities Hours: -National Call Center: 6am-1pm -Local Office Phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-3:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon -Local Office Walk in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-2:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon Email (503) 412-4750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) meetings and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) classes. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans; all discharge types Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CENTRAL OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. PO Box 7462 Bend, OR 977087462 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (541) 316-0167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS MULTNOMAH NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 228-5692 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE SERVICE NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides service navigation to connect veterans to support groups, housing resources, and apply for benefits. 2055 Union Avenue Suite 2 North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must be Veterans Affairs Grant Per Diem (VA-GPD) eligible and pass a criminal background check Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (541) 808-9025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic program including peer support for one's spiritual beliefs and practices; space and ceremonies (smudging, healing and pipe ceremonies, pow wows, sweat lodge, etc.), culturally competent practitioners and support systems, and education, cultural awareness, and advocacy. 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Individuals who are veterans and identify as Native American Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 273-5187 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK SOUTHERN OREGON VA REHABILITATION CENTER AND CLINICS NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic program including peer support for one's spiritual beliefs and practices; space and ceremonies (smudging, healing and pipe ceremonies, pow wows, sweat lodge, etc.), culturally competent practitioners and support systems, and education, cultural awareness, and advocacy. 8495 Crater Lake Highway White City, OR 97503 Eligibility: Individuals who are veterans and identify as Native American Hours: Varies by service (541) 826-2111 3391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VA ROSEBURG HEALTHCARE SYSTEM NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic program including peer support for one's spiritual beliefs and practices; space and ceremonies (smudging, healing and pipe ceremonies, pow wows, sweat lodge, etc.), culturally competent practitioners and support systems, and education, cultural awareness, and advocacy. 913 NW Garden Valley Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Individuals who are veterans and identify as Native American Hours: Varies by service (541) 440-1000 44333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETERAN ADVOCATES OF ORE-IDA ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers advocacy, support, and connection by providing space for camaraderie and conversation, a thrift store, military merchandise sales, treat boxes, quilts, cookies for troops, disposal and retirement of old flags, visitation to veterans who are housebound or in nursing homes, and opportunities to amplify veteran voices and stories. 180 W Idaho Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Veterans, service members, and their families. Volunteering and support is unrestricted. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 889-1978 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS FORT KENNEDY VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE SERVICE NAVIGATION VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK NATIVE AMERICAN SERVICES FOR VETERANS VETERAN ADVOCATES OF ORE-IDA ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT FOR VETERANS
